background.attention_bias: sequence, custody breaks, and admission decisions

readers.thesis: Source sovereignty begins when Foundation admits immutable source through a quarantined, reconstructable custody chain instead of treating a provider branch as canonical.
observations: Earlier runs separate execution, release, and recovery authority from providers.
The architecture requires offline export and second-operator recovery.
uncertainties: The current repository has not yet passed a full offline bundle intake laboratory.

background.experience: Git object databases, repository repair, and migration
background.attention_bias: object integrity, hidden refs, and transport assumptions
background.counter_bias: signed commits as sufficient source trust

readers.thesis: Source intake accepts an object graph only after strict integrity checks, local signature verification, reference capture, quarantine scanning, and an immutable normalization manifest.
observations: Git object validity and author identity are independent claims.
Submodules, external file pointers, hooks, and archives expand the intake attack surface.
uncertainties: Git SHA-256 interoperability remains incomplete across some ecosystems.
Pattern scanning cannot prove absence of sensitive material.

agreements: Provider verification badges never replace local verification.
Admission occurs before checkout, build, or hook execution.
Offline export and raw bundle custody preserve the exit path.
disagreements: Reader A prioritizes custody and operator approvals; Reader B prioritizes object-graph completeness and parser attack surfaces.
omissions_detected: No production intake gateway currently emits a canonical manifest.
No measured maximum bundle size or inspection throughput exists.
revisions_after_discussion: Gate intake on both strict object verification and two-person admission.
Keep signatures as identity evidence while retaining separate content and policy checks.

notes_added.statement: Treat every incoming repository as hostile data until Foundation verifies the complete object graph, signatures, references, and quarantine policy without executing repository-controlled code.
Make the admitted source identity an immutable manifest and retained offline bundle, never a provider branch name or verification badge.

notes_challenged.challenge: Reproducible builds remain downstream; they cannot repair unverified or incompletely captured source intake.

privacy.review: Contains only abstract architectural conclusions, synthetic reader profiles, coverage boundaries, and non-identifying continuity findings. Excludes transcript excerpts, personal facts, health or animal details, credentials, locations, deleted text, and unique conversation fragments.
